items.title: Times Square prepares for Sunday's World Cup final
items.description: Sunday's World Cup final between Spain and Argentina promises to be a classic, pitting Europe against South America and rising star Lamine Yamal against Lionel Messi. Spain head into the showdown unbeaten in 37 matches, while Argentina have built a reputation for producing some of the tournament's most dramatic comebacks. Selina Sykes looks ahead to the highly anticipated final.

items.title: Indian activist on hunger strike forcibly removed by police

items.title: 'Dangerously close to becoming normalised': Government responds to Islamophobia report

items.title: Ethiopia: New refugee support centre opens in Addis Ababa
items.description: The UN refugee agency and the Ethiopian government have opened a new support centre in Addis Ababa to provide refugees with free legal advice and access to essential services. The initiative aims to help refugees navigate bureaucracy, obtain vital documents and improve their chances of finding work. Ethiopia hosts more than one million refugees, making it Africa's third-largest refugee-hosting country. Tom Canetti reports from Addis Ababa.

items.title: ‘Our bodies contain poison’: Landmark trial in Argentina probes dangers of agrochemicals
items.description: Trial over agrochemical spraying in the city of Pergamino has set an unprecedented legal benchmark: for the first time, a federal court placed the entire agricultural production chain in the dock, from farmers and agronomists to public officials. Trial ends in acquittal for nearly all those excused, except two municipal employees. Leer más

items.title: Canada hopes to sign Mercosur free-trade deal this year

items.title: Why is Latin America shifting to the right?
items.description: 🇨🇴 🇵🇪 #Colombia and #Peru recently elected right-wing, #Trump-aligned leaders in close contests, which analysts link to weakening centrist parties and growing concern over crime rather than a clear ideological shift. 🇧🇷 Trump has amplified the trend by hosting regional allies in Florida, though his tariffs on #Brazil had the opposite effect, boosting leftist President Lula's standing. 🌎 🗳️ Brazil's October election will be a key test of whether the region's rightward trend continues.

items.title: Russian online retail warehouses hit by deadly Ukrainian strikes
items.description: Drones targeted Wildberries facilities near Moscow and in Tambov. Ukraine's leader called them "major logistics facilities" supplying "sanctioned components".

items.title: Fight or Flight: Trump administration subpoenas journalists over Air Force One reporting
items.description: This week France 24's media show "Scoop" looks at Donald Trump's latest attempt to intimidate the press. The New York Times broke the story that the Qatari-donated Air Force One had security concerns. It was clearly newsworthy: the US President flying on a plane that was less secure than another that was available to him. The administration responded by issuing subpoenas to the journalists who wrote the article. Our guest is Will Creeley from the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression.

items.title: US Democrats divided over support for Israel ahead of midterm elections

Ahead of November's midterm elections, the vote reflects a sharp erosion in support for Israel among left-leaning voters after years of war in Gaza.

items.title: Ethiopian Drill explodes on the streets of Addis Ababa
items.description: Last year, the aggressive genre of Drill could only be heard on social media in Ethiopia. But over the last few months the fast-paced, often matched with violent lyrics, style of hip hop has exploded on the streets of Addis Ababa, taking over mainstream bars and nightclubs. For some, its rapid popularity is due to Gen Z's lack of political and economic certainty. But Ethiopia remains a very conservative country, and not everyone is a fan of Drill. This report by our correspondent Tom Canetti.

items.title: France: 2,000 hectares lost in forest fires
items.description: France has been battling a series of devastating wildfires following record-breaking heatwaves, with the government saying the country has not experienced so many fire outbreaks since World War II. One of the largest blazes has scorched thousands of hectares in the historic Fontainebleau forest, south of Paris. In response, the Fondation du Patrimoine has launched a fundraising campaign to help restore the damaged woodland by replanting the forest. Elitsa Gadeva reports.

The 35-year-old has been widely credited with contributing to Ukraine's recent successes in its war with Russia, making his removal a source of significant public backlash.

Regional al Qaeda affiliate JNIM and the separatist Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) claimed responsibility for the attack in separate statements that cited "great human losses".
